description:
Problem: agents doing triage on the ready queue hit tasks that should plainly be deleted (e.g. duplicates of another open ball, or nonsense/stale tasks from a past exploration). They see no 'delete' command in SKILL.md, get spooked by 'Do not delete .balls/ files manually', and fall back to 'bl update X status=deferred'. Over time 'deferred' turns into a graveyard nobody revisits — it's indistinguishable from 'we decided not to do this' and 'I was afraid to close it'.

What actually exists today:
- 'bl update X status=closed --note "dup of Y"' works on an unclaimed task: it archives the file in one atomic state-branch commit via close_and_archive(). The task disappears from the ready queue and land in the archive.
- 'bl link add X duplicates Y' marks the relationship (non-blocking).
- Closed tasks are archived, not gone — the state-branch history still has them, so 'closing' is recoverable and not destructive in the way 'delete' sounds.

So the missing piece is docs, not code. SKILL.md should:
1. Name 'close' as the way to remove a task that shouldn't exist — with a concrete example for duplicates: 'bl link add X duplicates Y && bl update X status=closed --note "dup of Y"'. Call out that close archives rather than deletes, so it is safe.
2. Define 'deferred' narrowly: a task we've *decided* not to do *now*, with an intent to revisit. Not a synonym for 'I don't want to touch this'. If you aren't going to revisit it, close it.
3. Update the 'Not delete .balls/ files manually' warning so agents don't read it as 'never remove tasks' — the warning is about the on-disk store format, not about the task lifecycle.

Placement: the existing 'Task Lifecycle' and 'Commands' sections are the natural homes. A short 'Removing tasks' subsection near the lifecycle diagram would also work.

Acceptance:
- SKILL.md has a clear, findable paragraph on how to remove a duplicate/unwanted task, with the exact command.
- 'deferred' has a one-line definition that distinguishes it from 'closed'.
- No behavior changes in the binary; this is docs-only unless we discover a real code gap along the way.
title: SKILL.md: 'deferred' is not a trash can; close duplicates instead
